content:
First, label the $3\times3$ grid by “parity‐classes” in the usual checkerboard way: the five cells with $i+j\equiv0\pmod2$ (namely the four corners and the centre) vs.\ the four cells with $i+j\equiv1\pmod2$ (the side‐centres).  Any two edge‐adjacent cells have opposite checkerboard colour, so forcing all adjacent numbers to have opposite parity is equivalent to putting all 5 odd numbers into the 5 “even‐sum” cells and all 4 even numbers into the 4 “odd‐sum” cells.

Hence the total number of raw labellings is
\[
5!\times4!\;=\;120\cdot24\;=\;2880.
\]

We must now quotient by the four rotations of the square.  By Burnside’s Lemma the number of orbits is
\[
\frac1{4}\bigl(\#\text{fixed by }0^\circ
\;+\;\#\text{fixed by }90^\circ
\;+\;\#\text{fixed by }180^\circ
\;+\;\#\text{fixed by }270^\circ\bigr).
\]
Clearly
– under the identity ($0^\circ$) all $2880$ labellings are fixed,  
– under a $90^\circ$ (or $270^\circ$) rotation the four corners form a 4‐cycle (and the four edges another 4‐cycle), so one would have to repeat a label 4 times—impossible with distinct numbers ⇒ $0$ fixed,  
– under $180^\circ$ the corners pair up and the edges pair up ⇒ again would force duplicates ⇒ $0$ fixed.

Thus the total of fixed labellings is $2880+0+0+0=2880$, and the number of rotational‐inequivalent Isthmian arrangements is
\[
\frac{2880}{4}\;=\;\boxed{720}.
\]

message.content:
First, label the $3\times3$ grid by “parity‐classes” in the usual checkerboard way: the five cells with $i+j\equiv0\pmod2$ (namely the four corners and the centre) vs.\ the four cells with $i+j\equiv1\pmod2$ (the side‐centres).  Any two edge‐adjacent cells have opposite checkerboard colour, so forcing all adjacent numbers to have opposite parity is equivalent to putting all 5 odd numbers into the 5 “even‐sum” cells and all 4 even numbers into the 4 “odd‐sum” cells.

Hence the total number of raw labellings is
\[
5!\times4!\;=\;120\cdot24\;=\;2880.
\]

We must now quotient by the four rotations of the square.  By Burnside’s Lemma the number of orbits is
\[
\frac1{4}\bigl(\#\text{fixed by }0^\circ
\;+\;\#\text{fixed by }90^\circ
\;+\;\#\text{fixed by }180^\circ
\;+\;\#\text{fixed by }270^\circ\bigr).
\]
Clearly
– under the identity ($0^\circ$) all $2880$ labellings are fixed,  
– under a $90^\circ$ (or $270^\circ$) rotation the four corners form a 4‐cycle (and the four edges another 4‐cycle), so one would have to repeat a label 4 times—impossible with distinct numbers ⇒ $0$ fixed,  
– under $180^\circ$ the corners pair up and the edges pair up ⇒ again would force duplicates ⇒ $0$ fixed.

Thus the total of fixed labellings is $2880+0+0+0=2880$, and the number of rotational‐inequivalent Isthmian arrangements is
\[
\frac{2880}{4}\;=\;\boxed{720}.
\]
